author: Sangharakshita
title: Contents of Pali Canon - Ratana Sutta
content:
THE RATANA SUTTA SEMINAR

CONTENTS



Introduction - Sutta Nipata
Verse 1. Calling all beings
Verse 2. Seeing everything as alive
Setting up goodwill with the rest of the universe
Offerings to the Buddha. Stealing the perfume
Verse 3. The Buddha is the Jewel
The power of truth
Other worlds
The reading of verse 4.
Verse 4. Cessation
Verse 5. Deathlessness.
Preparation for meditation
The Dharma as growth.
Verse 6. The eight individuals, the Four pairs
The first three fetters
Customs of accruing merit
Verse 7. Sasana
The individual and choice
(Needs and greeds!)
Passion is passive, happiness is active
Seeing the needs of the objective situation Personal needs
Taking responsibility
Verse 8. Imperturbability
The Buddha's experiencing bodily suffering Sense consciousness is not unskilful
Developing insight -dhyana
Doctrine follower, faith follower
Verse 9. Stream Entry and rebirth
The fetters and the seven rebirths
Reactivity and Creativity
Leading a structured life
Verse 10. The indulgence of rights and ceremonies The need for transcendental sanctions within the group
Contrasts in social and domestic life - India and England
The four states of misery and the six crimes
Verse 11. Openness within the spiritual community
Verse 12. The spontaneous blossoming of the Dharma The sheer excellence of the
Dharma
Communicating the Dharma
Solitude
Verse 13. After insight - creativity
Leaving the past behind
The seeds of existence
Chanting used as spells and charms
Bhante's views on marriages and blessings
Challenging people's assumptions
General comments.
